title: Racinaea dielsii

intro: Racinaea dielsii, also known as the Andean air plant, is a species of epiphytic bromeliad native to the high-altitude Andean regions of South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, providing relief from respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory effects. The bioactive constituents of Racinaea dielsii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. Racinaea dielsii is commonly prepared as a tea or tincture, and is also used in traditional medicine to treat respiratory conditions, as well as to boost immune function and reduce inflammation.
